rationale: Next-phase integration looks consumable. The remediated spec now gives planning a hard prerequisite gate: both 109 and 110 must be merged to main, and 111 must be rebased or merged-forward before plan approve. The codex audit log path extends the 110 verdict filename in the same review directory without colliding with `<slot>-round-<N>.json`. The anti-laundering ladder is mutually exclusive enough for implementation: any rendered content, including a partial verdict followed by quota exhaustion, goes through same-reviewer reserialization or MISSING, while claude-sub is reserved for no-verdict quota only and obeys the flag. `ALLOWED_CLI` is implementable as a workflow-side command enum/helper for agent prompts; the grep AC is a smoke proof, with the exact-set requirement still binding in R5. Scope covers the required workflow, embed/setup, ownership, validation, and skill work. Advisory notes below should be carried into planning, but I do not see a spec-approve blocker.

findings.note: Round-1 R6 had no asks. Re-verification at bd898dfa still passes the next-phase integration lens.

Spec line 147 is precise: 111 may enter plan approve only after both 109 and 110 have merged to main and the 111 branch is rebased or merged-forward onto that post-109/110 main. That prevents planning from starting while 109 is merely plan-approved or 110 merely spec-approved.

The audit artifact `<spec-dir>/reviews/<slug>/<slot>-round-<N>.codex.log` is in the same directory and shares the same slot/round stem as the 110 verdict file `<slot>-round-<N>.json`. The `.codex.log` suffix is unambiguous for a bead author and does not compete with verdict JSON discovery.

Rendered malformed output, still-unparseable-after-retry, no-verdict quota with flag true, and no-verdict quota with flag false are separated cleanly. A codex stream that emits a partial verdict and then hits quota has rendered verdict content, so it is covered by R3 steps 1-2, not R4 substitution.

The R5 requirement is implementable if the workflow has a single agent-command helper or enum that only admits `ALLOWED_CLI` entries. The AC is only a string-presence smoke test and does not mechanically prove exact-set enforcement, but the requirement and falsified clause are explicit enough for a bead and review to enforce.

The required source surfaces have Scope and AC coverage: workflow copies, embed, Claude setup install, ownership claim, validate test, and the two plugin skill edits. The phrase `and their .claude/skills/** mirrors` should be handled carefully in planning because this worktree currently tracks plugin panel skills, while `.claude/skills` appears to be an install target for other skills rather than existing panel mirrors.
Requirement `3b` is acceptable. It preserves existing R4-R9 references and is not ambiguous for planning.
The stricter literal ban on `mindspec complete` plus `ALLOWED_CLI` is acceptable. Planning should make exact allowlist enforcement explicit rather than relying only on the grep AC.
Decoupling the untracked research document is acceptable; the spec now states the rationale directly instead of depending on absent quoted sections.
